The Art of Writing Clean Code

By Code Artisan · January 2024

Clean code is not about following rigid rules. It's about communicating intent clearly through your code. Every variable name, every function signature, every module boundary is an opportunity to make your code more readable.

The best code reads like well-written prose. It tells a story that other developers — including your future self — can follow without a map.

Here are three principles that guide clean code:

  1. Names should reveal intent
  2. Functions should do one thing
  3. Comments should explain why, not what
